message:
feat(frankenpandas): add SqlInsertMethod to prelude (fd90.220 / br-75rs3)

SqlWriteOptions has `pub method: SqlInsertMethod` (Single | Multi —
matches pandas to_sql(method='multi')) but the enum was only at the
top-level re-export, not in the prelude.

User code building SqlWriteOptions from the prelude could not set
this field:
    let opts = SqlWriteOptions {
        method: SqlInsertMethod::Multi,  // unreachable
        ...
    };

Same hidden-enum pattern as fd90.219 (CsvOnBadLines).

Fix:
- Add SqlInsertMethod to crate::prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -181,6 +181,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-715r","title":"pandas: fp-frame DataFrameGroupBy.agg dict-of-list per-column multi-func parity","description":"py signature:
df.groupby('k').agg({'a': ['sum', 'mean'], 'b': ['min', 'max']}) -> DataFrame

Current DataFrameGroupBy.agg accepts HashMap<String, String> (one func per column). Pandas lets the dict value be a list of funcs, producing MultiIndex columns ({col}_{func}). Add agg_dict_list (or extend agg) that takes &HashMap<String, Vec<String>> and emits one output column per (input_col, func) pair with {col}_{func} naming.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:50:55.384214272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:11:57.036828894Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:11:57.035031090Z","close_reason":"shipped e39f896: DataFrameGroupBy::agg_dict_list(HashMap<String, Vec<String>>) with 5 tests (per-column funcs, single-func, empty-list rejection, group-key rejection, column-order preservation). Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-73d0","title":"fp-columnar Column.between parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:49:54.493090546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:54.031356170Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:54.029499184Z","close_reason":"Deferred to keep the follow-on queue focused","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-75el","title":"fp-columnar Column.diff_valid (consecutive non-null diff) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:00:48.117815433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:19.326309991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:19.324496418Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::diff_valid in commit d4f2267, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-75rs3","title":"fd90.220: Add SqlInsertMethod to prelude","description":"fd90.220: Add SqlInsertMethod to the prelude.

SqlInsertMethod (Single/Multi) is the type of `pub method: SqlInsertMethod` field on SqlWriteOptions. README documents `pd.to_sql(.., method='multi')` semantics for the multi-row VALUES batching path. SqlWriteOptions is in the prelude (fd90.206), but SqlInsertMethod was not — same defect family as fd90.219 (CsvOnBadLines).

User code:
    let opts = SqlWriteOptions { method: SqlInsertMethod::Multi, ... };

cannot resolve SqlInsertMethod from prelude alone, even though the consuming SqlWriteOptions is reachable.

Scope:
- Add SqlInsertMethod to the prelude (top-level already has it via fp_io re-export at line 76)
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 5-10min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:14:21.598457702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:16:08.038526259Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:16:08.036498107Z","close_reason":"Added SqlInsertMethod (Single/Multi) to the prelude. Same pattern as fd90.219 (CsvOnBadLines): SqlWriteOptions has 'pub method: SqlInsertMethod' but the enum was reachable only at top-level (not in prelude). User code building SqlWriteOptions { method: SqlInsertMethod::Multi, ... } from the prelude could not resolve the enum. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-76mw","title":"fd90.40: SqlInspector::reflect_table for bundled table metadata","description":"Add SqlReflectedTable struct (table_name, columns, primary_key_columns, indexes, foreign_keys, unique_constraints, comment) and SqlInspector::reflect_table(table, schema) -> Result<Option<SqlReflectedTable>, IoError> that bundles all 5 introspection calls into one fetch. Mirrors SQLAlchemy.MetaData.reflect_table(). Returns Ok(None) when the table doesn't exist (i.e. table_schema returns None). Pure-delegation slice — calls table_schema, primary_key_columns, list_indexes, list_foreign_keys, list_unique_constraints, table_comment in sequence. Tests cover unknown table -> None, real SQLite table populating columns + PK + index, multi-schema PG-like routing, comment propagated through bundle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:10:09.123798115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:13:14.391172165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:13:14.388687912Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-78ttq","title":"fd90.149: README claims 30+ FP-P2D packets through 055; actual is 419+ packets through 433","description":"fd90.74-148 README audit. Conformance Testing in Depth section at line 1449 claims:

  '... plus 30+ DataFrame-level packets (FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055) covering merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more.'

But the actual fp-conformance fixture packet count for P2D is much higher:
  ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE 'fp_p2d_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l
  → 419 distinct P2D packet IDs

And the range:
  earliest: fp_p2d_014
  latest:   fp_p2d_433 (verified with sort -u | tail)

So:
- '30+ DataFrame-level packets' → actually 419+
- 'FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-055' → actually 014 through 433

The README's range claim caps at 055 — off by a factor of ~10× on count and ~7-8× on range.

Fix: update both the count ('30+' → '400+') and the range ('014 through 055' → '014 through 433+') to reflect actual coverage. The list of operations covered ('merge, concat axis options, head/tail, loc/iloc, sort, constructor variants, and more') is fine.

Pure README change. No code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:14:13.323083764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:15:41.153093405Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:15:41.149639554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-79wko","title":"fd90.147: frankenpandas Cargo.toml description still says '7 IO formats' — README + badge say 8 (fd90.89)","description":"fd90.74-146 audit. crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml package description (line 3) says:
  description = 'Clean-room Rust reimplementation of the pandas API — DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling, Resample, MultiIndex, 7 IO formats. Zero unsafe.'

But fd90.89 already updated the README from '7 IO formats' to '8 IO formats' (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/Feather/IPC/SQL) — the IPC stream format makes 8. The Cargo.toml description is stale and shows up on crates.io and 'cargo search', misleading users.

Fix: change '7 IO formats' to '8 IO formats' in the Cargo.toml description. Pure metadata change. No code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:03:17.940297405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:05:07.128257319Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:05:07.125626658Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
